title: Parameter validation in async/await methods should be wrapped
description: Parameter validation in "async"/"await" methods should be wrappedAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode Smellasync-awaitWhy is this an issue?Because of the wayasync/awaitmethods are rewritten by the compiler, any exceptions thrown during the parameters check will happen only when the task is observed. That could happen far away from the source of the buggy code or never happen for fire-and-forget tasks.Therefore it is recommended to split the method into two: an outer method handling the parameter checks (without beingasync/await) and an inner method to handle the iterator block with theasync/awaitpattern.This rule raises an issue when anasyncmethod throws any exception derived fromArgumentExceptionand containsawaitkeyword.Noncompliant code examplepublic static async
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects async methods that contain both argument validation (throwing ArgumentException family) and await keywords within the same method body
logic.triggering_conditions: Method is marked as async AND contains ArgumentException throwing AND contains await keyword
logic.exclusion_conditions: Test methods, methods with Assert/Console/Logger/Debug calls, generated code
logic.reasoning: Parameter validation in async methods should be separated from the async implementation to ensure immediate validation failure rather than deferred exceptions when the task is observed

should_trigger: public async Task<string> ReadFileAsync(string fileName) { if (fileName == null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(fileName)); return await File.ReadAllTextAsync(fileName); }
should_not_trigger: public Task<string> ReadFileAsync(string fileName) { if (fileName == null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(fileName)); return ReadFileAsyncCore(fileName); }
